commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9d4cd653b827a5b84e38fadedaabbc796c9b33ef)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a TrayMenuWindow-scoped ToggleSwitch control template, centralizes tray toggle construction/state updates, and routes the gateway and permissions toggles through the helper.

Reproducibility: yes. for the introduced blocker by source inspection: PR head puts the style in `RootGrid.Resources` but looks it up through `Application.Current.Resources` before using the helper from tray-menu build paths. I did not run the Windows tray UI in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because the PR has a concrete runtime resource lookup blocker and missing real tray-menu proof.

target.title: Fix tray menu toggle flicker
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a tray-menu-local `ToggleSwitch` style that preserves the WinUI switch shape and theme resources while removing the default reposition animation that flickers on menu open.
- Route the gateway connection and permissions flyout toggles through a shared helper so state updates stay consistent.
- Keep the style scoped to `TrayMenuWindow` to avoid broad app-wide control template changes.

## XAML review notes
- Uses existing `ToggleSwitch*` theme resources for Light/Dark/High Contrast behavior.
- Keeps the WinUI toggle dimensions, radii, and brushes; only removes the transition storyboard path.
- Scoped to the tray menu surface instead of global app resources.

## Validation
- `./build.ps1`
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-restore`
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore`
target.author: kenehong
